flow.title: Authentication data flow
flow.description: User registration or login; client obtains JWT.
flow.notes: Gateway routes /auth/* to Auth Service. Auth Service reads/writes Users DB and returns JWT.

flow.title: WebSocket connection and presence registration
flow.description: Client opens WebSocket for real-time messaging; JWT validated at upgrade. Presence registered so Messaging can resolve online/offline later.
flow.notes: Client passes JWT in query e.g. /ws?token=... Gateway proxies upgrade to Load Balancer. Channel Service validates JWT, extracts userId, calls Presence Service POST /presence/register. Heartbeat every 15s (batched). On disconnect: POST /presence/unregister.

flow.title: Send message (one-to-one or group)
flow.description: Client sends a message over existing WebSocket; real-time delivery and async persistence.
flow.notes: Client is already connected via API Gateway → Load Balancer → Channel Service. JWT was validated at WebSocket upgrade. No Gateway in per-message path. Messaging resolves recipients (from channels/channel_members elsewhere), then Presence for online subset; offline = all − online.

flow.title: Message history data flow
flow.description: Client fetches chat history via HTTP GET.
flow.notes: Gateway validates JWT and routes GET /messages/:channelId to Messaging Service. Messaging Service reads from Messages DB. Auth Service and Users DB are not in this path.

flow.title: List my channels (conversations)
flow.description: Client requests list of channels (conversations) for the current user.
flow.notes: Gateway validates JWT and routes e.g. GET /users/:userId/channels or GET /channels to Messaging Service. Messaging Service queries Channels DB (channels joined by user) and returns list.

flow.title: Create channel (DM or group)
flow.description: Client creates a new channel (direct message or group).
flow.notes: Gateway validates JWT and routes e.g. POST /channels (type, name, memberIds) to Messaging Service. Messaging Service writes to channels and channel_members in Channels DB and returns channelId.

pattern.name: circuit_breaker
pattern.description: After a threshold of failures (e.g. 5 in 10 seconds), stop calling the downstream service; fall back to a safe behavior. After cooldown, try one request (half-open); if success, close circuit.
pattern.purpose: If Presence is down, avoid hanging or wasting retries; fail fast and fall back.
pattern.fallback: Assume recipient offline; push to Notification Queue so offline user gets email.

pattern.name: retry_exponential_backoff_jitter
pattern.description: On transient failure, retry with exponential backoff (e.g. baseDelay * 2^attempt) plus jitter (random offset) so many clients don't retry at the same time. Cap with maxDelay.

applies_to.call: Channel Service → Presence Service (register, unregister, heartbeat)

pattern.purpose: Handle network blips and short outages without failing the request.

pattern.name: outbox
pattern.description: Messaging Service writes message to an outbox table in the same DB transaction instead of pushing directly to Message Queue and Redis Pub/Sub. A separate Outbox Publisher process polls the outbox and publishes to the queue and Pub/Sub. Guarantees at-least-once publish; requires idempotent consumers.

applies_to.component: Outbox Publisher (polls outbox, publishes to Message Queue and Redis Pub/Sub)

pattern.purpose: Avoid partial failure (e.g. message in queue but not Pub/Sub, or vice versa).
pattern.note: Adds latency to real-time path (poll interval); implement only if strong consistency is required.

pattern.name: inbox
pattern.description: Messaging Processor Worker keeps a record of processed message IDs (e.g. in a table or cache). Before writing a message to Messages DB, check if the ID was already processed; if yes, skip (idempotent consumption).
pattern.purpose: Safe to receive the same message twice from the queue (at-least-once delivery); no duplicate rows in Messages DB.
pattern.note: Pairs naturally with outbox (outbox can publish duplicates; inbox deduplicates).
